title: Software Should Work Worked
excerpt: Recap of Software Should Work from the organizer's perspective
content_html:
Isaac Van Doren

2026-07-23

Not only did Software Should Work work, it went better than I could have ever possibly imagined.

During most conference talks there's a large contingent of attendees who opt to skip the session and chat instead. Talking with other attendees is a great use of time at a conference, but as the organizer I was naturally a bit worried about this: what if half of the attendees skip the talks and the energy isn't there? Much to my surprise, almost everyone watched every talk! Further, I'm used to looking around during conference talks and seeing many attendees buried in their laptops or phones, daydreaming, or just ready for the talk to be over. Not so at SSW. Every time I turned around to look at the crowd I was pleasantly surprised; everyone was paying attention!

Having such a present, engaged audience led to a wonderful feeling of cohesion. The speakers kept referencing each other's talks in their own. You could talk to any attendee about any of the talks and know they had seen it. I have never heard so many successful jokes at a conference. I knew some of the speakers would be funny, but I really didn't expect that they all would! This only happened because the audience was so engaged.

The vibes were just right. We could agree that software is often broken and that we have the power to make it excellent. Sometimes discussions around quality devolve into despair around the sorry state of the status quo; not so at SSW. There was an overarching feeling of optimism and great positive energy towards building better software.

Two days after the conference, Andrew Kelley opened an issue on the Zig repo to add support for a Fil-C inspired compilation mode. Andrew was right there in the audience during Fil's (incredible) talk on Fil-C. It's easy to think abstractly that organizing a conference will "promote innovation" or something, but wow; to think that SSW might play a tiny role in such an epic feature being added to a major programming language?!

A core reason that the event worked is that we had such high-quality attendees. You couldn't walk three steps without bumping into someone working on something interesting who might as well have been a speaker. It seemed like everyone there completely got the premise of the conference: "Yeah, software should work!".

Throughout the organizing process, many people asked me "Why are you hosting this event in Columbia, Missouri?!". The simple answer is that I live here. However, this choice of location turned out to be a feature, not a bug. Because Columbia isn't a major hub, it acted as a natural filter: the people who showed up were the ones that really wanted to be there.

Fortunately Columbia is also an excellent setting for a conference. You can't go wrong with so many great restaurants, coffee shops, and bars within a short walk of the venue. Plus the trail system, plus Mizzou's campus, plus all the art, etc. One attendee, Grigoris, flew in all the way from Greece. He had some spare time before heading back, so he and my dad ended up going sailing.

I could not be happier with how this all went. Thank you to everyone who provided support along the way.

Filip Pizlo's talk on Fil-C will be the first recording to be published on our YouTube channel. It includes easily the best live demo I've ever seen. Today at 2pm CT; you don't want to miss it!

content_html_zh:
艾萨克·范·多伦

2026-07-23

Software Should Work 不仅有效，而且比我想象的还要好。

在大多数会议演讲期间，都有大量与会者选择跳过会议并进行聊天。 在会议上与其他与会者交谈是一种很好的利用时间的方式，但作为组织者我自然有点担心这一点：如果一半的与会者跳过演讲而没有精力怎么办？ 令我惊讶的是，几乎每个人都观看了每一次演讲！ 此外，我习惯于在会议演讲期间环顾四周，看到许多与会者埋头于笔记本电脑或手机中，做白日梦，或者只是准备演讲结束。 但在 SSW 却并非如此。 每次我转身看向人群时，我都会感到惊喜；大家都在关注！

拥有这样的礼物，全神贯注的观众产生了一种美妙的凝聚力。 演讲者在自己的演讲中不断引用对方的演讲。 您可以与任何与会者谈论任何演讲，并知道他们已经看过。 我从来没有在会议上听过这么多成功的笑话。 我知道有些演讲者会很有趣，但我真的没想到他们都会！ 这只是因为观众如此投入而发生的。

气氛恰到好处。 我们同意软件经常会被破坏，但我们有能力使其变得优秀。 有时，关于质量的讨论会演变成对现状的绝望。 SSW 则不然。 人们对构建更好的软件抱有一种总体的乐观情绪和巨大的积极能量。

会议两天后，Andrew Kelley 在 Zig 存储库上提出了一个问题，以添加对 Fil-C 启发的编译模式的支持。 在 Fil 关于 Fil-C 的（令人难以置信的）演讲中，安德鲁就在观众席中。 人们很容易抽象地认为组织会议将“促进创新”或其他什么，但是哇；认为 SSW 在添加到主要编程语言中的如此史诗般的功能中可能扮演微小的角色吗？！

该活动成功的一个核心原因是我们拥有如此高质量的参与者。 你不可能走三步而不碰到正在做一些有趣的事情的人，而他也可能是一位演讲者。 似乎每个人都完全明白了会议的前提：“是的，软件应该可以工作！”。

在整个组织过程中，很多人问我“为什么要在密苏里州哥伦比亚举办这个活动？！”。 简单的答案是我住在这里。 然而，这种位置选择被证明是一个功能，而不是一个错误。 由于哥伦比亚不是主要枢纽，因此它起到了天然过滤器的作用：出现的人是真正想去那里的人。

幸运的是，哥伦比亚也是举办会议的绝佳场所。 从酒店步行不远便可抵达众多一流的餐厅、咖啡馆和酒吧，绝对不会出错。 加上步道系统，加上密苏里大学的校园，加上所有的艺术品，等等。一位与会者，格里戈里斯，从希腊专程飞来。 回去之前他有一些空闲时间，所以他和我爸爸最后去航海了。

我对这一切的进展感到非常高兴。 感谢一路上提供支持的所有人。

Filip Pizlo 关于 Fil-C 的演讲将是我们 YouTube 频道上发布的第一个录音。 它轻松地包含了我见过的最好的现场演示。 今天下午 2 点（中部时间）；你不想错过它！
